sourceMovie.title: Speed
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Speed for the same reason it broke through in 1994: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Jan de Bont keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Die Hard
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Speed clicked for you, Die Hard (1988) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Quantum of Solace
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Speed clicked for you, Quantum of Solace (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Speed?
faq.answer: Try Die Hard, Quantum of Solace, Die Hard: With a Vengeance—each shares DNA with Speed while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Speed?
faq.answer: Speed (1994) is best described as Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Speed part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Speed Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
